definition: A very large expanse of salt water covering most of the earth.
A large natural elevation of the earth's surface rising abruptly.
A large natural stream of water flowing to the sea or a lake.
A building or room containing a collection of books to read or borrow.
A person qualified to treat people who are ill.
A person who helps others to learn, especially in a school.
A two-wheeled vehicle that you ride by pushing pedals.
A folding device you hold over your head to keep off the rain.
A very large grey mammal with a trunk and tusks.
A sweet sticky substance made by bees.
The coldest season of the year.
A surface that reflects an image of whatever is in front of it.
A stick of wax with a wick that gives light when burned.
A piece of ground where flowers, fruit, or vegetables are grown.
A soft cushion used to support the head while sleeping.
A machine that converts energy into motion.
The process or season of gathering crops.
The loud sound that follows a flash of lightning.
An instrument that shows direction using a magnetic needle.
A heavy object dropped to keep a ship from drifting.
An instrument used to view distant objects, especially in space.
A mountain that can erupt with lava and ash.
A dry, barren area of land with little rainfall.
A piece of land completely surrounded by water.
An arch of colours in the sky caused by light through rain.
One of the light structures that cover a bird's body.
A dark shape produced when something blocks light.
To speak very softly using one's breath.
An act of travelling from one place to another.
A quantity of precious metals, jewels, or valuable objects.
A structure built to cross over a river, road, or gap.
A large fortified building, typically from the medieval period.
A portable case with transparent sides protecting a light.
A sheltered area of water where ships can anchor safely.
A field of grass and wildflowers.
A slowly moving mass of ice formed from packed snow.
The line where the earth or sea seems to meet the sky.
An object that attracts iron and produces a magnetic field.
A game or problem designed to test ingenuity.
A structure of rungs used for climbing up or down.
A container with a spout used for boiling water.
A thin pointed tool used for sewing.
A seat fastened on a horse's back for a rider.
The full moon nearest the autumn equinox.
A military stronghold, especially a strongly fortified town.
A hard crystalline rock used in sculpture and building.
A large stringed instrument played by plucking.
A brass instrument played by blowing into a mouthpiece.
A tool with two blades for cutting.
A tool with a heavy head used for driving nails.
A flexible bag filled with air or gas that floats.
A chart showing the days, weeks, and months of a year.
A flat paper container for a letter.
The physical property of attraction shown by magnets.
The force that pulls objects toward the earth.
The curved path of an object around a star or planet.
The preserved remains of a prehistoric organism.
A substance in the gas phase that is usually liquid or solid.
A sound that is reflected back to the listener.
Molten rock that erupts from a volcano.
An icy body that releases gas and dust, forming a tail near the sun.
A huge system of stars, gas, and dust held together by gravity.
A weight hung so it can swing freely, used to regulate movement.
A curved mirror in a reflecting telescope that gathers light.
A machine for reaping and gathering crops.
A small stringed instrument played with a bow.
Sympathetic concern for the suffering of others.
The ability to do something that frightens one.
The capacity to accept delay or trouble without getting angry.
The quality of being truthful and free of deceit.
The power to act, speak, or think as one wants.
The quality of having good judgement based on knowledge.
A pleasing combination or arrangement of parts.
A strong desire to know or learn something.
The quality of being thankful.
A strong desire to achieve something.
A sequence of musical notes that is satisfying to hear.
A strong, regular repeated pattern of sound or movement.
Complete absence of sound.
A place giving protection from danger or weather.
A deep-sea fish that produces its own light.
A gentle wind.
A thin layer of ice formed on surfaces in cold weather.
The first appearance of light in the sky before sunrise.
The darker stage of twilight after sunset.
A low area of land between hills or mountains.
A steep rock face, especially at the edge of the sea.
A large cave or chamber underground.
An area of low, wet, marshy ground.
A large area covered chiefly with trees.
A very small mouse that lives among tall grasses.
A tower with a bright light to warn or guide ships.
A building with sails turned by wind to grind grain or pump water.
A structure that sends a jet of water into the air.
Able to extend or be seen at a distance, as with a telescope.
Feeling or showing sympathy for others.
A precious stone of pure carbon, the hardest natural substance.
A bright green precious stone.
A transparent blue precious stone.
A celebration of the food grown on the land.
A small body of matter that burns brightly as it enters the atmosphere.
An object that orbits a planet, natural or artificial.
A body of water or air moving in a definite direction.
